justification: As the operator, A delivers more usable intelligence per page. Coverage favors A — it surfaces the failed beachfront food-truck tender (March 12, 66,000 PLN starting bid), legacy closures (Malika, Trafik, North Fish), competitor delivery promos, dietary tagging gaps, and a concrete Meta Ads white space, plus a priced delivery comparison across five competitors. B's edge is the hours-disconnect call-out (Monsun closing 8/9 PM during Open'er) and pedestrianization-permit angle, both genuinely operational, but B leans heavier on premise-restating prose. Specificity goes to A on dated events and named prices; source diversity also A (29 vs 20 domains, including delivery, municipal, press, festival sites). Specialist-set correctness clearly favors B: it dispatched exactly the expected market_landscape + marketing_digital + dynamic_researcher_1 trio, while A added location_traffic and menu_pricing. Actionability is roughly even — A's marketing tactics versus B's permit/hours moves. Holistically, A wins.
